title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will occur from July 11-16, featuring the Sail250 fleet with vessels from over 25 countries.
Queen Elizabeth II visited Boston for the 1976 event, marking the first British monarch's visit accompanied by Prince Philip.
Past Sail Boston events in 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017 drew millions, overcoming protests and celebrating maritime history.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel attends Patriots rookie minicamp, bringing renewed energy after offseason controversy.
Vrabel's culture-building credited for Patriots' rebound and crucial for 2026 success against tough schedule.
New safety Kevin Byard confident in Vrabel's unchanged coaching style, recalling his tenure in Tennessee.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive two-run homer in the eighth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 3-2 win.
Payton Tolle pitched a career-best eight innings, allowing two earned runs and striking out three batters.
Aroldis Chapman secured the save despite a bases-loaded situation, capping Boston's first comeback win after seven innings in 2026.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story placed on 10-day IL with a sports hernia, possible surgery being considered by the Red Sox.
Infielder Nick Sogard called up from Triple-A Worcester to replace Story on the Red Sox roster.
Story's struggles include a .206 batting average, 32.4% strikeout rate, and six errors this season.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old driver died in a head-on collision with a school bus in Marlborough, NH, Friday morning.
The school bus carried 13 students from Monadnock Regional Middle High School; none were seriously injured.
The incident is under investigation by New Hampshire State Police and local authorities; witnesses are urged to contact investigators.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit led the Braves to a 3-2 victory over the Red Sox in the 10th inning.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy pulled pitcher Connelly Early after five innings, despite him wanting to continue.
The Red Sox failed to capitalize on scoring opportunities, going 1-for-3 with runners in scoring position and leaving seven on base.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots unlikely to reunite with Stefon Diggs despite mutual interest due to pending A.J. Brown trade.
The Patriots are set to absorb A.J. Brown's $32 million AAV contract, complicating salary cap room for Diggs.
Diggs had a 1,000-yard receiving season last year and reportedly seeks a one-year, $10 million deal.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen near 965 Blue Hill Ave. in Dorchester on Tuesday afternoon.
Boston Police describe him as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, often seen in areas like Columbia Road and Harambee Park.
Authorities urge anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives or submit anonymous tips via CrimeStoppers.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, is accused of shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The female victim, 42, sustained life-threatening injuries; the male victim, also 42, had non-life-threatening wounds.
Broomes, charged with assault and firearm offenses, is held without bail pending a May 21 dangerousness hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Former Red Sox players, including Chris Sale and Mookie Betts, excel on other teams with Betts batting .171.
Jared Carrabis highlights five former Red Sox pitchers with a combined 2.74 ERA in 2026.
Team of former Red Sox players includes starters like Rafael Devers, Xander Bogaerts, and Kyle Schwarber.
